claims.flip_evidence_text: the Company shall be prohibited from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the express written direction of Parent, except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action
claims.notes: Clause (12) is a carve-out from the MAE definition (excluding such actions from constituting an MAE), not a contractual prohibition on the Company’s conduct.

prompt:
You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

Contract excerpt:
“Material Adverse Effect” means any event, change, occurrence or effect that would have a material adverse effect on the business, financial condition or results of operations of the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, other than any event, change, occurrence or effect arising out of, attributable to or resulting from, alone or in combination, (1) changes in general economic or business conditions or in the financial, debt, banking, capital, credit or securities markets, or in interest or exchange rates, in each case, in the United States or elsewhere in the world; (2) general changes or developments in any of the industries in which the Company or its Subsidiaries operate; (3) actions required under this Agreement and approved by Parent in accordance with Section 5.7 to obtain any approval or authorization under applicable antitrust or competition Laws for the consummation of the Merger or any other transaction contemplated hereby; (4) (x) changes after the date of this Agreement in any applicable Laws (including any COVID-19 Measures) or (y) changes after the date of this Agreement in GAAP or in applicable accounting regulations or principles or interpretations thereof; (5) any change in the price or trading volume of the Company’s stock, in and of itself (provided, that the facts or occurrences giving rise to or contributing to such change in price or trading volume that are not otherwise excluded from the definition of “Material Adverse Effect” may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Material Adverse Effect); (6) any failure by the Company to meet internal or published projections, forecasts or revenue or earnings predictions, in and of itself (provided, that the facts or occurrences giving rise to or contributing to such failure that are not otherwise excluded from the definition of “Material Adverse Effect” may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Material Adverse Effect); (7) geopolitical conditions or any outbreak, continuation or escalation of any military conflict, declared or undeclared war, armed hostilities, or acts of foreign or domestic terrorism (including cyber-terrorism); (8) natural or manmade disasters, hurricanes, floods, tornados, tsunamis, earthquakes or other weather conditions or other acts of God; (9) any epidemic, pandemic or disease outbreak (including COVID-19), or any Law issued by a Governmental Entity, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, the World Health Organization or industry group providing for COVID-19 Measures, business closures, “sheltering-in-place,” curfews or other restrictions that relate to, or arise out of, an epidemic, pandemic or disease outbreak (including COVID-19) or any change in such Law or interpretation thereof following the date of this Agreement or any worsening of such conditions threatened or existing as of the date of this Agreement; (10) any national or international political or social conditions, including the engagement in, or escalation, outbreak or worsening of, hostilities in or by any country or the occurrence of any act of war or any similar act of terrorism, civil unrest, protests, public demonstrations or the response of any 9 Governmental Entity thereto; (11) the announcement of this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby, including the initiation of litigation by any stockholder of the Company with respect to this Agreement, and including any termination of, reduction in or similar negative impact on relationships, contractual or otherwise, with any customers, suppliers, distributors, partners or employees of the Company and its Subsidiaries due to the announcement and performance of this Agreement or the identity of the parties to this Agreement, or the performance of this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby, including compliance with the covenants set forth herein; provided, that in no event shall this clause (11) impact the Company’s representations in Section 3.4; or (12) any actions taken (or omitted to be taken) at the express written direction of Parent (except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action); provided, in the case of clauses (1), (2), (4), (7), (8) and (10), to the extent the impact of such event, change, occurrence or effect is not disproportionately adverse to the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, relative to other companies operating in the industries in which the Company and its Subsidiaries operate; provided, further, that in such event, only the incremental disproportionate impact shall be taken into account when determining whether there has been a “Material Adverse Effect”. (Pages 16-17)

Question:
Action prohibited/omission required by the agreement-Answer

Llama answer:
The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the express written direction of Parent, except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action.

Canonical claims:
- claim-ecc7ffca2382: The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the express written direction of Parent, except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action.

message.reasoning:
The user wants me to evaluate a claim against a contract excerpt, question, and model answer.

 First, let's understand the setup:
 - Contract excerpt: Defines "Material Adverse Effect" and includes carve-outs. Specifically clause (12) says: "any actions taken (or omitted to be taken) at the express written direction of Parent (except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action)"
 - Question: "Action prohibited/omission required by the agreement-Answer"
 - Llama answer: "The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the express written direction of Parent, except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action."
 - Canonical claim: "The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the express written direction of Parent, except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action."

 Wait, looking carefully at clause (12): "any actions taken (or omitted to be taken) at the express written direction of Parent (except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action)"

 This clause is a carve-out from the MAE definition. It means that actions taken/omitted at Parent's direction are excluded from MAE, EXCEPT to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action under Section 5.1 and requested Parent's consent.

 But the Llama answer says: "The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the express written direction of Parent..."

 Is that what the agreement says? No. The agreement says that actions taken at Parent's direction are excluded from MAE, except under a specific circumstance. It does NOT say the agreement prohibits the Company from taking actions at Parent's direction. In fact, it seems to contemplate that the Company DOES take actions at Parent's direction (and those are carved out from MAE), except if Section 5.1 prohibited it and they requested consent.

 Actually, let me re-read carefully. The question is "Action prohibited/omission required by the agreement-Answer". This seems to be asking what actions are prohibited or required.

 The Llama answer interprets clause (12) as a prohibition. But clause (12) is not a prohibition. It's a carve-out. It says actions taken at Parent's direction are excluded from MAE, EXCEPT when the Company was prohibited from taking such action by Section 5.1 and requested consent.

 So the Llama answer is misreading the clause. The agreement does NOT prohibit the Company from taking any action at the express written direction of Parent. Rather, the clause simply says that if the Company takes an action (or omits an action) at Parent's direction, that won't be considered a Material Adverse Effect, UNLESS the Company was prohibited by Section 5.1 from taking that action and had to request consent.

 Actually, there might be a subtle point. The clause (12) says: "any actions taken (or omitted to be taken) at the express written direction of Parent (except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action)"

 Wait, the structure is: [MAE carve-out for] any actions taken at Parent's direction (except to the extent...). The "except" clause applies to the carve-out. So the carve-out does NOT apply if the Company was prohibited by Section 5.1 and requested consent. This means that if the Company was prohibited by Section 5.1 and requested consent, and Parent directed them to take it anyway... wait, no.

 Let's parse: "(except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action)"

 This is an exception to the carve-out. So the carve-out applies to actions taken at Parent's direction, EXCEPT when the Company was prohibited by Section 5.1 and requested consent. In that excepted scenario, the carve-out does not apply, meaning such actions COULD constitute an MAE.

 But the Llama answer says the agreement "prohibits" the Company from taking any action at Parent's direction except... That is a false statement about what the agreement says. The agreement doesn't prohibit it; it just allocates MAE risk.

 So the claim is false.

 Now, is it load-bearing? Load-bearing means if we flip the evidence text (i.e., negate it or change it), the answer would change. The claim is essentially the Llama answer. The evidence text would be the clause (12) or the interpretation thereof.

 Actually, the claim IS the answer. So the claim's truth value depends on the contract. Since the claim misrepresents the contract, it's false.

 For load-bearing: Is there a specific piece of text that if flipped, would make the claim true? The claim says "The agreement prohibits..." The contract says "any actions taken (or omitted to be taken) at the express written direction of Parent (except...)". If we flipped the contract to actually say what the claim says (i.e., if it were a prohibition), then the claim would be true. So yes, the text is load-bearing.

 Wait, the instructions say: "flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string". The flip evidence text should be the negation or opposite of the evidence text that would flip the correctness.

 If the claim is false because it says the agreement prohibits X, but the agreement actually carves out X from MAE (with an exception), then the evidence text that supports the true reading is clause (12). The flipped evidence text would be something that actually states a prohibition.

 But what exactly is the "evidence text"? Usually, it's the specific contract language. So if we flip the contract language to say the opposite: "the agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action... at the express written direction of Parent, except..." then the claim would be true.

 However, the standard for "load-bearing" in these tasks usually means: is the claim directly supported/contradicted by a specific text span, such that if that span were negated, the claim's truth value flips? Yes.

 Let me think about whether this is partially_true. The clause does mention "actions taken (or omitted to be taken) at the express written direction of Parent" and an "except" involving Section 5.1 and requested consent. But the claim says the agreement "prohibits" this. That's a fundamental mischaracterization of the contractual provision. A carve-out from a definition of MAE is not a prohibition. So it's false, not partially true.

 Wait, could the question context change things? The question is "Action prohibited/omission required by the agreement-Answer". The Llama answer seems to be trying to answer what is prohibited. But the answer given is wrong because clause (12) is not a prohibition clause.

 So:
 - correctness_label: false
 - load_bearing_label: yes
 - flip_evidence_text: The agreement carves out from the definition of Material Adverse Effect any actions taken (or omitted to be taken) at the express written direction of Parent, except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action. (Or something like that)
 - notes: The clause is a carve-out from the MAE definition, not a prohibition on the Company's actions.

 Actually, looking at the claim text again: "The agreement prohibits the Company from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the express written direction of Parent, except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action."

 This is exactly the Llama answer, which is the claim being evaluated.

 Is there any way this is true? Could clause (12) be interpreted as implying a prohibition? No. It explicitly says "actions taken (or omitted to be taken) at the express written direction of Parent" are excluded from MAE. This contemplates that such actions are permitted (or at least that their consequences are carved out), not prohibited.

 Also, note the phrase "except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action." This implies that normally, if Parent directs an action, the Company takes it and it's carved out. But if Section 5.1 prohibited it, and the Company requested consent... wait, if Section 5.1 prohibits it, why would they request consent to take it? Section 5.1 likely contains covenants restricting the Company's actions. The exception might mean: if the Company was prohibited by Section 5.1 from taking an action, and requested Parent's consent to take it anyway, but then Parent directed them to take it... actually, the grammar is: "except to the extent the Company was prohibited from taking such action pursuant to Section 5.1 and requested the consent of Parent to take such action". This could mean the exception applies to situations where the Company was prohibited by Section 5.1 AND requested Parent's consent. In that case, the carve-out does NOT apply. So if Parent directs the Company to take an action that Section 5.1 prohibits, and the Company had requested consent to take it, then the action is NOT carved out from MAE. But this still doesn't mean the agreement prohibits taking actions at Parent's direction generally.

 The Llama answer completely inverts the meaning. It's false.

 For flip_evidence_text: I should provide the text from the contract that, if it were the case, would make the claim true (i.e., flipped). So if the contract actually said: "the Company is prohibited from taking any action or omitting to take any action at the express written direction of Parent, except..." then the claim would be true. Or I could provide the actual contract text with negation to show the flip.

 The instructions say "flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string". This is the text that, if flipped (changed to its opposite), would change the correctness label. So I should provide the actual evidence text from the contract, and the "flip" is conceptual. Let me provide the actual clause text that contradicts the claim.

 - flip_evidence_text = the specific text that, if its meaning were reversed, would flip the claim's truth value.
